title: Undercut-overcut and pit-wave pressure features from same-lap rivals
summary: Public F1 strategy analysis emphasizes that pit timing depends on nearby rivals, warm-up penalties, and whether a local pit wave is starting.
rationale: Public F1 strategy analysis emphasizes that pit timing depends on nearby rivals, warm-up penalties, and whether a local pit wave is starting. The current best features are mostly self-state and finish-window heuristics, not opponent-triggered opportunity cost.
implementation_hint: Add leakage-safe race-lap context features using only current-lap information: number of nearby-position rivals within 2s-equivalent rank band, count of same-compound cars older than you, same-lap field pit count, whether adjacent rivals just pitted last lap, and soft/hard warm-up favorability proxies by race-level degradation regime.
expected_effect: Likely adds orthogonal signal for tactical stops that are driven by cover/attack behavior rather than absolute tyre exhaustion.
risk: Without true time-gap telemetry these are proxies; crude approximations may be noisy on circuits where track position matters differently.
